title: Bignonia costata
taxonomy: Bignonia costata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as the Bignonia family. It belongs to the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, lavender, and honeysuckle. The family Bignoniaceae is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world and consists of over 800 species. Bignonia costata is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is characterized by its distinctive, compound leaves and showy, trumpet-shaped flowers.
uses: Bignonia costata is known to have various medicinal properties in traditional medicine. Its bark is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's leaves are also used in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues. In some regions, the plant is used to treat malaria due to its antimalarial properties. Further research is required to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses.
